MCP server 1: Pre-built MCP connectors framework for 50+ popular SaaS tools including GitHub, Slack, Jira, Linear, Notion, Asana, Google Drive, HubSpot, and more. TypeScript/Zod-based library powering the disco.dev platform. Supports both Tools and Resources capabilities with per-connector credential management. Runs on Bun, Node, and Cloudflare Workers via a stateless StreamableHTTP server.
MCP server 2: A secure intermediary between AI language models and Keeper Secrets Manager (KSM) enabling AI agents to perform secrets management tasks including listing, retrieving, searching, creating, updating, and deleting secrets and folders. Supports file attachments via upload and download, password generation with TOTP code retrieval, and confirmation workflows for sensitive write operations to safeguard enterprise application credentials.
MCP server 3: A hosted Model Context Protocol server enabling developers to interact with MercadoLibre APIs using natural language, simplifying product integrations and task automation across Latin America's largest e-commerce marketplace. Accessible via a remote HTTPS endpoint with Bearer token authentication and supports MercadoLibre developer API resources and utilities.
MCP server 4: A Model Context Protocol server integrating with the TrackMage API for shipment tracking and logistics management across 1600+ carriers worldwide. Provides tools for creating and managing shipments and orders, retrieving tracking checkpoints, detecting carriers from tracking numbers, and listing available carriers and workspace orders. Supports OAuth 2.1 client credentials authentication via STDIO transport.
MCP server 5: A Model Context Protocol client for interacting with the Odos DEX aggregation platform and Zerion portfolio analytics across multiple blockchain networks. Provides tools for retrieving swap and zap quotes, token pricing, supported chain and liquidity source data, wallet portfolio composition, historical transactions, gas price estimates, and smart contract information. Odos tools require no authentication while Zerion portfolio tools require a ZERION_API_KEY.
MCP server 6: A Model Context Protocol server for managing Ceph clusters through the Croit REST API using natural language commands. Dynamically generates tools from Croit's extensive OpenAPI specifications, enabling cluster management across services, pools, servers, and S3 object storage with log search capabilities and automatic token optimization that reduces AI processing costs by 80-95%.
MCP server 7: A Model Context Protocol server for querying Axiom observability and log analytics data using Axiom Processing Language (APL). Provides tools to execute APL queries against datasets, retrieve dataset schemas, access saved queries, and view monitoring configurations and execution history. Note: this repository is archived and no longer maintained as of March 2026; users should migrate to the official Axiom MCP server at mcp.axiom.co.
MCP server 8: Jira Service Management MCP Server by CData provides read-only access to live Jira Service Management data through natural language queries using CData JDBC Driver. Exposes JSM data as SQL-queryable tables via MCP.
MCP server 9: eBay MCP Server by CData provides read-only access to live eBay data through natural language queries using CData JDBC Driver for eBay. Exposes eBay data as SQL-queryable tables via MCP interface.
MCP server 10: Stadia Maps MCP Server provides AI assistants with access to Stadia Maps APIs for location intelligence including geocoding, routing, isochrone analysis, and static map generation. It exposes six tools covering time zone queries, forward and bulk geocoding, route overviews with encoded polylines, reachability contours, and PNG map image rendering with markers and route overlays.

MCP server 1: Resources are supported per connector (verified in apps/server/src/index.ts via server.resource() calls). disco.dev reports 388+ Tools & Resources total across all 50 connectors. Specific resource URIs vary by connector. Example connector resources expose read-only data endpoints (e.g., repository metadata, user profiles, configuration data) accessible via MCP resource URIs.
MCP server 8: • {mcpScheme}/{tableName} – List of columns for table (resource type: text)
MCP server 9: • {prefix}://{tablePath} – Table column schema resource returning catalog, schema, table, column name, and data type as text/csv (one resource registered per available eBay table)
